creator: dclarke@blastwave.org
text:
The kernel module from ports/virtualbox-ose-kmod seems to be working well 
with the pkg and thus : 


titan # 
titan # make deinstall
===>  Deinstalling for virtualbox-ose-kmod
===>   Deinstalling virtualbox-ose-kmod-5.2.12
Updating database digests format: 100%
Checking integrity... done (0 conflicting)
Deinstallation has been requested for the following 1 packages (of 0 packages in the universe):

Installed packages to be REMOVED:
        virtualbox-ose-kmod-5.2.12

Number of packages to be removed: 1
[1/1] Deinstalling virtualbox-ose-kmod-5.2.12...
[1/1] Deleting files for virtualbox-ose-kmod-5.2.12: 100%
titan # 
titan # make reinstall
===>  Installing for virtualbox-ose-kmod-5.2.18
===>   Registering installation for virtualbox-ose-kmod-5.2.18
Installing virtualbox-ose-kmod-5.2.18...
titan # 
titan # /usr/sbin/pkg query -e '%a = 0' '%o'
security/ca_root_nss
ports-mgmt/dialog4ports
ports-mgmt/pkg
emulators/virtualbox-ose
emulators/virtualbox-ose-kmod
ftp/wget
titan # 

titan# /usr/sbin/pkg query -e '%a = 0' '%o'
security/ca_root_nss
ports-mgmt/dialog4ports
ports-mgmt/pkg
emulators/virtualbox-ose
emulators/virtualbox-ose-kmod
ftp/wget
titan# 

A look to /boot/loader.conf and /etc/rc.conf sorts out the rest and after
a reboot no panic is seen.  Testing now.
